state.topic: let's make a mmo about shipping food and stuff from shop to buyer with shipper (3 player roles)

checklist.label: Objective
checklist.value: Build a cross-platform (mobile-first, web-supported) food delivery MMO game where 3 player roles (Shop, Buyer, Shipper) interact through a real-world map interface with in-game currency, live order tracking, and a ratings/reviews system.

checklist.label: Scope
checklist.value: MVP includes: (1) Username-only authentication with role switching (Shop/Buyer/Shipper); (2) Shop: create/manage food menu with names, prices, images; (3) Buyer: browse shops/menus, place orders, pay with in-game virtual currency, live-track shipper on map, accept delivery, rate/review shipper and shop; (4) Shipper: view available orders, accept orders, navigate real-world map (OSM/Leaflet) for pickup and delivery with real-time location broadcasting; (5) Order lifecycle with basic safety nets (auto-expire, buyer cancel before accept, no shipper cancel mid-delivery); (6) Ratings and reviews for both shippers and shop food.

checklist.label: Non-goals
checklist.value: No leaderboard; no real money/payment processing; no real GPS tracking (manual addresses); no non-food items; no email/password auth (username-only for MVP); no shipper mid-delivery cancellation; no admin dashboard; no push notifications.

checklist.label: Constraints
checklist.value: Backend: Node.js with TypeScript; mobile-first, cross-platform (web + mobile); real-world map via OpenStreetMap/Leaflet (free, no API key); real-time communication via WebSockets; in-game virtual currency only; single codebase for all platforms.

checklist.label: Success criteria
checklist.value: A user can: (1) Sign up with username and switch between Shop/Buyer/Shipper roles; (2) As Shop: create a menu with items/prices; (3) As Buyer: browse shops, place an order, pay with virtual cash; (4) As Shipper: accept the order, navigate map for pickup/delivery with the Buyer seeing live position updates; (5) Buyer receives and accepts delivery; (6) Buyer rates/reviews shipper and shop food. Full end-to-end flow works with multiple concurrent users.

checklist.label: Evidence required
checklist.value: (1) All backend API endpoints working (verified via tests or manual curl); (2) WebSocket real-time tracking functional — shipper position updates visible to buyer; (3) End-to-end walkthrough: user can play all 3 roles in sequence on the same account; (4) Multiple concurrent users can interact (e.g., Buyer A orders from Shop B, Shipper C delivers); (5) Order state machine transitions correctly through all states; (6) Basic safety nets trigger correctly (auto-expire, cancel before accept).

checklist.label: Risks
checklist.value: (1) Real-time WebSocket reliability — disconnections mid-delivery could corrupt order state; (2) Map integration complexity — Leaflet/OSM tile loading and coordinate management; (3) Cross-platform UI consistency — mobile-first design may not translate perfectly to desktop web; (4) Concurrent order conflicts — multiple shippers accepting same order; (5) In-memory state vs database — need to decide persistence strategy early; (6) Scope creep — food delivery app is feature-rich, temptation to add beyond MVP.

checklist.label: Edge cases
checklist.value: Orders auto-expire after a time limit if unclaimed; buyers can cancel before a shipper accepts (full refund); shippers cannot cancel mid-delivery; what if no shipper is online (order stays pending until timeout); what if buyer disconnects during live tracking (reconnect and resume); what if shipper disconnects mid-delivery (order state preserved, shipper can reconnect).

checklist.label: Affected files / technical context
checklist.value: Greenfield project at C:/Users/mnguyen/source/repos/spf-mmo — empty repo with only .pi directory. No existing code, patterns, or constraints. Fresh Node.js/TypeScript project to be created from scratch.

answers.question: What platform and tech stack do you have in mind for this MMO?
answers.answer: Both web app and mobile app, mobile-first approach. No specific tech stack preference — wants recommendation.

answers.question: Real-time vs asynchronous gameplay?
answers.answer: Mixed: async order placement, real-time live tracking during delivery.

answers.question: What does each role do and how does a transaction flow?
answers.answer: Same as Shopee Food / Uber Eats. Shop lists items, Buyer orders, Shipper picks up and delivers. Key: shipper must navigate a world map to deliver.

answers.question: What makes this an MMO/game vs just a delivery platform?
answers.answer: Real-world map (Google Maps/OpenStreetMap) with light gamification — points, ratings, badges, leaderboards.

answers.question: MVP scope: real money vs in-game currency, real GPS vs manual addresses?
answers.answer: In-game currency only (no real money), fake/manual addresses (no real GPS tracking).

answers.question: What's the MVP success criteria?
answers.answer: Full food delivery flow: browse shops → order → pay with virtual cash → shipper picks up → live tracking → deliver → accept → rate/review shipper and shop food. No leaderboard in MVP.

answers.question: Can one account play multiple roles?
answers.answer: One account can be all 3 roles — switch between them in the app.

answers.question: Authentication method for MVP?
answers.answer: Simple username-only login (no passwords needed for MVP).

answers.question: Edge cases: cancellations, timeouts, no shipper available?
answers.answer: Basic safety nets: orders auto-expire after time limit; buyers can cancel before shipper accepts; shippers can't cancel mid-delivery.

answers.question: What can shops sell? Shop menu management?
answers.answer: Food only. Shops can freely create and manage their own menus (add items, set prices).

goalContract.objective: Build a cross-platform (mobile-first, web-supported) food delivery MMO game where 3 player roles (Shop, Buyer, Shipper) interact through a real-world map interface with in-game currency, live order tracking, and a ratings/reviews system.
scope: Username-only authentication with role switching (Shop/Buyer/Shipper) from a single account
Shop role: create and manage food menu (items, prices, images)
Buyer role: browse shops/menus, place orders, pay with in-game virtual currency, live-track shipper on map, accept delivery, rate/review shipper and shop
Shipper role: view available orders, accept orders, navigate real-world map (OSM/Leaflet) for pickup/delivery with real-time location broadcasting
Order lifecycle with basic safety nets: auto-expire, buyer cancel before shipper accepts, no shipper mid-delivery cancellation
Ratings and reviews for both shippers and shop food
nonGoals: No real money or payment processing
No real GPS tracking (manual addresses only)
successCriteria: User can sign up with username and switch between all 3 roles
As Shop: create menu with items and prices
As Buyer: browse shops, place order, pay with virtual cash
As Shipper: accept order, navigate map for pickup/delivery with Buyer seeing live position updates
Buyer rates/reviews shipper and shop food
Full end-to-end flow works with multiple concurrent users
constraints: Real-world map via OpenStreetMap/Leaflet (free, no API key)
Greenfield project — no existing code to integrate with
evidenceRequired: All backend API endpoints working (tests or manual curl)
WebSocket real-time tracking functional — shipper position updates visible to buyer
End-to-end walkthrough: single user plays all 3 roles in sequence
Multiple concurrent users can interact (Buyer orders from Shop, Shipper delivers)
Order state machine transitions correctly through all states
Basic safety nets trigger correctly (auto-expire, cancel before accept)
risks: Real-time WebSocket reliability — disconnections mid-delivery could corrupt order state
Map integration complexity — Leaflet/OSM tile loading and coordinate management
Cross-platform UI consistency — mobile-first design may not translate perfectly to desktop web
Concurrent order conflicts — multiple shippers accepting same order
In-memory state vs database — need to decide persistence strategy early
Scope creep — food delivery app is feature-rich, temptation to add beyond MVP
suggestedSubgoals: 1. Project scaffolding: Initialize monorepo with Node.js/TypeScript backend, React/Next.js frontend, WebSocket server, and database schema
2. Auth & role system: Username-only login, role switching between Shop/Buyer/Shipper
3. Shop management: CRUD for shop menus (items, prices, images)
4. Buyer ordering: Browse shops, cart, place order, virtual currency payment
5. Shipper system: Order pool, accept orders, map navigation with Leaflet/OSM
6. Real-time delivery tracking: WebSocket-based shipper position broadcasting visible to buyer
7. Order lifecycle: State machine (pending → accepted → picked-up → in-transit → delivered), auto-expire, buyer cancel before accept
8. Ratings & reviews: Post-delivery rating/review for shipper and shop food
